playwright.use_when: Browser page workflows with DOM-visible navigation, forms, buttons, links, and page content.
playwright.avoid_when: Native OS dialogs, file pickers, desktop apps, browser chrome, and permission prompts.

native_accessibility.use_when: Desktop apps, OS UI, file pickers, browser chrome, and controls unavailable to browser automation.

terminal.use_when: Command-line workflows where shell commands and their output are the primary interface.

file_system.use_when: Direct file and folder operations that do not require interacting with a visible application.

visual_computer_use.use_when: Fallback for visible desktop interactions when browser automation or native accessibility cannot reliably address the control.
visual_computer_use.avoid_when: A structured browser or native accessibility control can perform the same action.

guidance: Prefer browser automation for page navigation, form fields, buttons, links, and DOM-visible controls.
Use native accessibility or visual computer-use for browser chrome, permission prompts, downloads, and OS dialogs.
Prefer platform-native accessibility controls for desktop app windows and OS UI.
On macOS use Accessibility API / AX; on Windows use UI Automation / UIA; on Linux use AT-SPI/accessibility APIs.
Use browser automation for web-page steps.
Use native accessibility for file pickers, desktop dialogs, browser chrome, and OS-level controls.
Use shell commands for terminal workflows and verify command output before continuing.
Use file-system operations for direct file changes.
Use native accessibility for file pickers or Finder/Explorer dialogs.
Start with structured native accessibility when available.
Fall back to visual computer-use if the surface cannot be identified.
